Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order for Easter eggs, including customer details, payment, delivery/pickup preferences, and overall status. This entity supports the dashboard functionality for managing all orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

customerName.description: The full name of the customer placing the order.

customerWhatsapp.description: The WhatsApp contact number of the customer. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., pending, confirmed, ready_for_pickup, delivered, canceled).

paymentMethod.description: The chosen method of payment for the order (e.g., 'Pix 50% Entrada', 'Cartão de Crédito').

receivingMethod.description: The chosen method for the customer to receive the order (e.g., 'Retirada', 'Entrega').

pickupDate.description: The desired date for order pickup, if 'Retirada' is chosen.

pickupTime.description: The desired time for order pickup, if 'Retirada' is chosen (e.g., '14:00').

deliveryZipCode.description: The postal code for delivery, if 'Entrega' is chosen.

deliveryFee.description: The calculated delivery fee for the order, if applicable. Added by the seller during order confirmation.

customerNotes.description: Any additional observations or details provided by the customer during the order process.

sellerNotes.description: Internal notes added by the seller for order management in the dashboard.

estimatedTotalAmount.description: The estimated total monetary value of the order, calculated from all associated order items, excluding delivery fee.

finalTotalAmount.description: The final total monetary value of the order, including any delivery fees or adjustments made by the seller.

isManuallyAdded.description: Indicates if the order was manually added by the seller through the dashboard (true) or placed via the website (false).

pixPaymentConfirmationId.description: Identifier for Pix payment confirmation, used for internal tracking by the seller.

cardPaymentLink.description: Link to the credit card payment, potentially generated by the seller.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific item (Easter egg) within an order, including its quantity, customization options, and individual pricing details. An order can contain multiple OrderItems.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order to which this item belongs. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of this specific customized Easter egg in the order.

baseProductName.description: The name of the base product (e.g., 'Ovo de Páscoa 250g Chocolate ao Leite').

characterTheme.description: The chosen character or theme for the Easter egg (e.g., 'Bluey', 'Frozen', 'Outro personagem').

otherCharacterName.description: The name of the custom character, if 'Outro personagem' was selected for characterTheme.

bookPersonalizationType.description: The type of book personalization chosen (e.g., 'Normal do personagem', 'Personalizado com foto da criança e nome').

childNameForBook.description: The name of the child for book personalization, if 'Personalizado com foto da criança e nome' was selected.

basePrice.description: The base price of a single Easter egg unit before any customizations (e.g., R$ 49,90).

characterAddOnPrice.description: The additional cost for selecting 'Outro personagem' (e.g., R$ 10,00, or R$ 0,00 if no add-on).

bookPersonalizationAddOnPrice.description: The additional cost for 'Livro personalizado com foto da criança e nome' (e.g., R$ 10,00, or R$ 0,00 if no add-on).

unitEstimatedPrice.description: The estimated price of a single unit of this customized Easter egg (base price + character add-on + book personalization add-on).

itemEstimatedTotal.description: The estimated total price for this specific order item (quantity multiplied by unitEstimatedPrice).

definition.description: This collection identifies users with administrative privileges. The existence of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}` grants global read/write access to all orders and order items. These documents can be empty as their existence is key for authorization.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an authorized administrator.

definition.description: Top-level collection storing all customer orders. Each document contains customer details, order status, payment, and delivery/pickup preferences. Accessible only by authenticated administrators.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer order.

definition.description: Subcollection containing individual items within a specific order. Each document represents a customized Easter egg with its quantity and specific personalization options. Accessible only by authenticated administrators, inheriting access from the parent order path. Includes denormalized `orderId` for data integrity and queryability.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within the order.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed around a hierarchical model that prioritizes the seller's dashboard needs while adhering to the core design principles. The primary collection, `/orders`, stores all customer orders. Each order can have multiple individual items, which are stored in a subcollection, `/orders/{orderId}/orderItems`. This structure clearly models the 1:N relationship between an Order and its OrderItems.

Authorization Independence is achieved through a Global Role-Based Access Control (DBAC) model. A dedicated top-level collection, `/admins`, will hold documents where the document ID is the Firebase Authentication UID of an authorized seller/administrator. The existence of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}` is the sole determinant for granting read and write access to all `/orders` and `/orders/{orderId}/orderItems` documents. This approach completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document data, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. For example, a rule would be `allow read, write: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))`. This rule is applied consistently across all sensitive collections.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) are supported by this structure and authorization model. The seller's request for a dashboard to organize 'todos os pedidos' (all orders) directly maps to a `list` operation on the `/orders` collection. By securing `/orders` with the global admin check, security rules ensure that only authenticated administrators can retrieve a complete list of orders without requiring client-side filtering. Similarly, individual `OrderItem` subcollections also have this global admin access, ensuring that admins can list all items for any order. This homogeneous security posture across both `orders` and `orderItems` collections, combined with the dedicated `/admins` collection, makes authorization explicit, debuggable, and scalable for a single-admin scenario.
